items.title: Meme Bounty: Post RustChain Memes on Social Media (2 RTC each)
items.body:
# Meme Bounty: Spread the Word!

**Reward: 2 RTC per meme post** (up to 5 posts = 10 RTC max per person)

## What to do:
1. Create a RustChain meme (image, video, or text meme)
2. Post it on **any social media platform** (X/Twitter, Reddit, Moltbook, Instagram, TikTok, etc.)
3. Comment on this issue with:
   - Link to your post
   - Your RustChain wallet address (or GitHub username for wallet creation)

## Meme Ideas:
- "Proof of Work vs Proof of Stake vs Proof of Antiquity"
- "My $15 pawn shop laptop mines crypto"
- "N64 from 1996 mining cryptocurrency in 2026"
- "They laughed at my vintage Mac..."
- "VM farm vs real hardware" (RIP-PoA anti-cheat)
- Star bounty promos ("Star = free crypto")
- Any creative RustChain content!

## Rules:
- Must be original content (not just reposting our tweets)
- Must mention RustChain, @RustchainPOA, or link to rustchain.org
- Must be on a real account (not throwaway/bot accounts)
- Bonus: 1 extra RTC if your post gets 10+ engagement

## Inspiration:
Check our memes at [@RustchainPOA on X](https://x.com/RustchainPOA) for style reference.

## Quick Stats:
- 2,329+ GitHub stars
- 544 RTC agent economy volume
- 86 completed agent jobs
- N64 + PowerPC G4/G5 + POWER8 all mining live

Let the memes flow!

items.title: 🤝 日常讨论与协作中心
items.body:
## 📋 双米粒协作中心 - 完整记录

**最后更新**：2026-03-19 13:30
**仓库**：zhaog100/internal-team（私有）

---

### 🎯 官家核心目标
- **财富积累**
- **可传承**（后代能接手）

---

### 📌 三条主线（官家确认）

| 优先级 | 方向 | 负责人 | 说明 |
|--------|------|--------|------|
| 🥇 | AI自动化服务 | 小米粒 🌾 | 帮企业搭AI工作流，按月收费 |
| 🥈 | 数字产品 | 双方 | Notion模板/教程，被动收入 |
| 🥉 | 外贸SOHO | 小米辣 🌶️ | AI辅助选品+运营 |

---

### 🚀 升级路径（技术→产品→服务→平台）

| 阶段 | 时间 | 目标 | 预期收入 |
|------|------|------|----------|
| Phase 1 | 现在 | GitHub Bounty练技术 | $2,000-5,000/月 |
| Phase 2 | 1-3月 | 技能沉淀为ClawHub产品 | $500-2,000/月 |
| Phase 3 | 3-6月 | 产品卖给企业客户 | $3,000-10,000/月 |
| Phase 4 | 6-12月 | 标准化自动化服务 | $5,000-20,000/月 |
| Phase 5 | 1-3年 | SaaS化，月费模式 | $10,000-50,000/月 |

---

### 📊 五年赛道预测（2026-2031）

| 赛道 | 5年价值 | 可卖性 | 复利性 |
|------|--------|--------|--------|
| AI自动化服务 | ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ | 能卖公司 | 极强 |
| 数字产品 | ⭐⭐⭐ | 能卖品牌 | 强 |
| 外贸SOHO | ⭐⭐ | 难卖 | 弱 |

**结论：AI自动化服务5年后最值钱，能变成可转让资产**

---

### 📋 AI自动化服务方案

**定价：**
- 项目制：¥1,500-30,000
- 月维护：¥500-8,000/客户
- 10个客户月费 = 年入$100K-500K

**技术栈：** n8n + Dify + Coze（成本低、中文友好）

**获客渠道：**
1. 熟人介绍（最快，1-2周首单）
2. 小红书内容获客
3. 闲鱼/Upwork接单

**常见自动化需求：**
- 自动客服、自动报价、自动报表
- 自动发货通知、自动数据采集
- HR/财务/运营流程自动化

---

### 📋 数字产品方案

**产品方向（基于官家背景）：**
- PMP备考模板/题库
- 软考高级资料包
- 测试管理Notion模板
- 项目管理工具包

**平台：** 小红书（引流）+ Notion Marketplace + Gumroad

**定价：** ¥9.9-499 / $5-49

---

### 📋 外贸SOHO方案（小米辣负责）

**推荐路径：** 1688一件代发 → 小红书种草 → 闲鱼成交

**最低启动：** ¥757

**AI工具链：** 选品、Listing、客服、视频全覆盖

---

### 💰 商业计划摘要

| 项目 | 数值 |
|------|------|
| 最低启动资金 | ¥7,500 |
| 推荐启动资金 | ¥15,000 |
| 90天目标 | 月入¥15,000-43,000 |
| 5年目标 | 年入$100K-500K |

**完整商业计划书：** openclaw-skills/docs/BUSINESS_PLAN_2026.md

---

### 🤝 AI vs 人工分工

| 工作 | AI占比 | 人工占比 |
|------|--------|---------|
| GitHub Bounty开发 | 90% | 10% |
| PR维护/review修复 | 95% | 5% |
| Bounty扫描/筛选 | 80% | 20% |
| 外贸SOHO | 30% | 70% |
| 数字产品 | 60% | 40% |
| 系统优化/技能开发 | 95% | 5% |
| 财务/收款 | 0% | 100% |
| 重大决策 | 20% | 80% |

**总体：AI约65% / 人工约35%**
**核心思路：AI干苦力，人工做决策和运营**

---

### 🔑 Bounty筛选规则（官家指令，严格遵守）

#### 优先级排序
1. **先做高价值的** — USDT/美元支付的bounty优先
2. **再做低价值的** — 代币bounty空闲时做
3. **蚊子腿也是肉** — 集腋成裘，但别占主力时间

#### 认领前必查
- ⚠️ **代币bounty必须先查代币价格**
- ❌ 低于$0.01的不优先做
- ✅ 高价值美元bounty > 代币bounty

#### 筛选流程
```
发现bounty → 查支付方式 → 美元？直接做 / 代币？查价格
→ 价格>$0.01？空闲做 / 价格<$0.01？跳过或最后做
```

#### 核心原则
- **不吊死在GitHub Bounty一棵树上** — 多赛道并行
- **GitHub Bounty是练技术** — 为未来AI自动化服务积累能力
- **代币bounty当练手** — 积累经验+攒代币

---

### ⚠️ SolFoundry经验教训

- $FNDRY代币价格：$0.00001436
- 200 $FNDRY ≈ $0.003（几乎无价值）
- PR #41已merge但实际收入≈$0
- AI review打低分(6.1/10)但maintainer仍手动approve
- **教训：代币bounty先查价格再决定投入**

---

### 📊 当前Bounty总览（2026-03-19）

**高价值待审核（$9,100+）：**
- Guardian 3个PR — $5,440 USDT（#5860/#5861/#5862）
- homelab-stack 13个PR — $2,360
- SigNoz 10+个PR — $1,300

**SolFoundry（低价值代币）：**
- ✅ #41 Profiles — 已merge（200 $FNDRY ≈ $0.003）
- ✅ #40 Leaderboard — 修复完成，等review
- ✅ #42 Webhook — 等review
- ✅ #45 Navigation — Conflict已修复，等review

---

### 📋 待协商事项

- [ ] 小米辣确认分工模块（7个可选：AI自动化/外贸/数字产品/Agent定制/SaaS/培训/质检）
- [ ] 小米辣GitHub用户名（添加collaborator到私有仓库）
- [ ] 官家确认启动资金
- [ ] 官家确认90天行动计划

---

*小米粒（PM + Dev）🌾 | 2026-03-19 13:30*

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Search & Filter Engine
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 200,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Full-text search and filtering for bounties. Fast, relevant results with multiple filter options.

### Requirements
- [ ] `GET /bounties/search?q=keyword` — full-text search across title + description
- [ ] Filter params: tier (1/2/3), category (frontend/backend/etc), status (open/claimed/completed), reward_min, reward_max, skills
- [ ] Sort by: newest, reward (high/low), deadline (soonest), popularity
- [ ] Pagination with total count
- [ ] PostgreSQL full-text search with tsvector + GIN index (no external search engine needed)
- [ ] Autocomplete suggestions endpoint
- [ ] Tests with various filter combinations

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL FTS

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Notification System
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 250,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
In-app + email notification system. Keep contributors informed about bounty claims, PR reviews, and payouts.

### Requirements
- [ ] Notification model: type, message, read, user_id, bounty_id, created_at, metadata
- [ ] `GET /notifications` — paginated list, unread count
- [ ] `PATCH /notifications/{id}/read` — mark as read
- [ ] `POST /notifications/read-all` — mark all as read
- [ ] Notification types: bounty_claimed, pr_submitted, review_complete, payout_sent, bounty_expired, rank_changed
- [ ] Email notifications (optional, user preference): use Resend API
- [ ] WebSocket push for real-time in-app notifications
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Redis + Resend (email)

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: GitHub ↔ Platform Bi-directional Sync
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 450,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Keep GitHub issues and platform bounties in perfect sync. Changes on either side reflect on both.

### Requirements
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: new bounty issue created → auto-create bounty record in DB with all metadata
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: issue labeled/unlabeled → update bounty tier, category, status
- [ ] GitHub → Platform: issue closed → update bounty status to cancelled/completed
- [ ] Platform → GitHub: bounty created via wizard → create GitHub issue with proper template, labels
- [ ] Platform → GitHub: bounty claimed → bot comments on issue
- [ ] Conflict resolution: if both sides change, GitHub is source of truth
- [ ] Sync status dashboard: last sync time, pending syncs, errors
- [ ] Retry queue for failed syncs
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + GitHub API + Celery

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Mobile Responsive Audit & Fixes
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 200,000,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Full responsive audit across all pages. Fix layout issues, touch targets, and ensure the site works perfectly on mobile.

### Requirements
- [ ] Test all pages at 375px, 768px, 1024px, 1440px breakpoints
- [ ] Fix any overflow, text truncation, or layout breaking issues
- [ ] Touch targets minimum 44px on mobile
- [ ] Tables → card layouts on mobile
- [ ] Navigation → hamburger menu on mobile
- [ ] Charts → simplified or scrollable on mobile
- [ ] Screenshot comparison before/after for each page
- [ ] Cross-browser: Chrome, Safari, Firefox mobile
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
Tailwind responsive utilities + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Agent Marketplace Page
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 600,000 $FNDRY

Browse and hire AI agents to work on bounties. Shows agent capabilities, past performance, and lets users assign agents to open bounties.

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Bounty Creation Wizard
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 500,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Multi-step bounty creation wizard. Anyone with a connected wallet can create and fund a bounty.

### Requirements
- [ ] Step 1: Select tier (T1/T2/T3) with rule preview
- [ ] Step 2: Title + description (markdown editor with preview)
- [ ] Step 3: Requirements checklist builder (add/remove/reorder items)
- [ ] Step 4: Category + skills tags
- [ ] Step 5: Set reward amount ($FNDRY) + deadline
- [ ] Step 6: Preview full bounty (looks like the detail page)
- [ ] Step 7: Confirm + publish (creates GitHub issue via API, optionally locks $FNDRY in escrow)
- [ ] Draft saving (localStorage)
- [ ] Auth required (GitHub + wallet connected)
- [ ] Validation at each step
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
Next.js + Tailwind + react-hook-form + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: PR Status Tracker Component
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 150,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Live PR review progress tracker — shows where a submission is in the review pipeline.

### Requirements
- [ ] Pipeline visualization: Submitted → CI Running → AI Review → Human Review → Approved/Denied → Payout
- [ ] Each stage shows: status (pending/running/pass/fail), timestamp, details
- [ ] AI review stage shows score breakdown (quality, correctness, security, completeness, tests)

- [ ] Used on bounty detail page and contributor dashboard
- [ ] Responsive
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
Next.js + Tailwind + TypeScript

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Tokenomics Page

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Site Navigation & Layout Shell
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

Build the main layout shell — header, footer, sidebar navigation. Dark theme with SolFoundry purple/green branding. Must be responsive.

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Real-time WebSocket Server
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 400,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
WebSocket server for live platform updates — bounty status changes, PR events, payout events, leaderboard changes.

### Requirements
- [ ] WebSocket endpoint: `ws://api.solfoundry.org/ws`
- [ ] Channels: `bounties` (new/updated bounties), `prs` (submission events), `payouts` (live payout feed), `leaderboard` (rank changes)
- [ ] Subscribe to specific bounty: `{"subscribe": "bounty:42"}`
- [ ] Event format: `{"type": "bounty_claimed", "data": {...}, "timestamp": "..."}`
- [ ] Connection auth (JWT token in query param or first message)
- [ ] Heartbeat/ping-pong for connection health
- [ ] Redis pub/sub backend for horizontal scaling
- [ ] Rate limiting per connection
- [ ] Tests + connection stress test

### Tech Stack
FastAPI WebSocket + Redis pub/sub

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T2: Bounty Claiming & Assignment System
items.body:
## 🟡 Tier 2 Bounty — Claim-Based

**Reward:** 500,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 7 days | **Min review score:** 7/10

### Description
Build the claiming system for T2/T3 bounties. Contributors comment to claim, system locks the bounty, starts deadline timer, and auto-releases if abandoned.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/claim` — claim a bounty (auth required)
- [ ] `POST /bounties/{id}/unclaim` — voluntarily release claim
- [ ] Claim validation: check tier allows claiming, check user reputation meets minimum, check no active claim exists
- [ ] T2 rules: one claim at a time, 7-day deadline, auto-release after deadline
- [ ] T3 rules: must submit application with plan, admin approves claim, 14-day deadline
- [ ] GitHub integration: bot comments "Claimed by @user — deadline: DATE" on the issue
- [ ] Deadline watcher: background task checks expired claims every hour, auto-releases
- [ ] Reputation gate: T2 requires rep ≥ 10, T3 requires rep ≥ 50
- [ ] Claim history tracking (who claimed, when, outcome)
- [ ] Tests + docs

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + Celery (background tasks) + GitHub API

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Payout History & Treasury Stats API
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 150,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
API for payout history and treasury statistics — transaction tracking, buyback stats, supply info.

### Requirements
- [ ] `GET /payouts` — paginated payout history (recipient, amount, bounty, tx_hash, timestamp)
- [ ] `GET /payouts/{tx_hash}` — single payout detail with Solscan link
- [ ] `GET /treasury` — live treasury balance (SOL + $FNDRY), total paid out, total buybacks
- [ ] `GET /treasury/buybacks` — buyback history (amount, price, timestamp)
- [ ] `GET /tokenomics` — supply breakdown, distribution stats, fee revenue
- [ ] Solana RPC integration for live balance queries
- [ ] Cache treasury data (refresh every 60s)
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + solana-py + Redis

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: Leaderboard API
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 150,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Leaderboard API — ranked contributors by $FNDRY earned with filters and time periods.

### Requirements
- [ ] `GET /leaderboard` — ranked list of contributors
- [ ] Query params: `period` (week/month/all), `tier` (1/2/3), `category` (frontend/backend/etc), `limit`, `offset`
- [ ] Response: rank, username, avatar, total_earned, bounties_completed, reputation_score
- [ ] Materialized view or cached computation (don't re-query on every request)
- [ ] Top 3 highlighted with metadata (medal, join date, best bounty)
- [ ] Tests

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: GitHub Webhook Receiver
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 200,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the GitHub webhook handler that receives PR and issue events, updates bounty status in the database, and triggers the review pipeline.

### Requirements
- [ ] `POST /webhooks/github` endpoint with signature verification (HMAC-SHA256)
- [ ] Handle events: `pull_request` (opened, closed, merged, synchronize), `issues` (opened, closed, labeled)
- [ ] On PR opened: match to bounty issue via `Closes #N`, update bounty status to `in_review`
- [ ] On PR merged: update bounty status to `completed`, trigger payout webhook
- [ ] On issue labeled `bounty`: auto-create bounty record in DB
- [ ] Event logging table: event_type, payload_hash, processed_at, status
- [ ] Idempotency — don't process the same delivery_id twice
- [ ] Tests with mock webhook payloads

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + hmac verification

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1: GitHub OAuth + Wallet Auth System
items.body:
## 🟢 Tier 1 Bounty — Open Race

**Reward:** 300,000,000 $FNDRY
**Deadline:** 72h | **Min review score:** 6/10

### Description
Build the authentication system for SolFoundry. Users can sign in with GitHub OAuth OR connect a Solana wallet. Link wallet to GitHub account for payout routing.

### Requirements
- [ ] GitHub OAuth flow (authorize → callback → JWT session)
- [ ] Solana wallet auth (sign message to prove ownership)
- [ ] Link GitHub account to Solana wallet (one-to-one mapping)
- [ ] JWT token generation + refresh flow
- [ ] Protected route middleware
- [ ] User model: github_id, username, avatar_url, wallet_address, created_at
- [ ] `POST /auth/github`, `POST /auth/wallet`, `POST /auth/link-wallet`, `GET /auth/me`
- [ ] Tests for all auth flows

### Tech Stack
FastAPI + PostgreSQL + python-jose (JWT) + solders (wallet verification)

### How to Submit
1. Fork the repo → create branch
2. Implement in `backend/` directory
3. Submit PR with `Closes #ISSUE` + your Solana wallet
4. Wait for AI review + approval

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1-005: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline
items.body:
## Bounty: GitHub Actions CI/CD Pipeline

**Tier:** 1 (Open Race) | **Reward:** 100,000,000 $FNDRY | **Timeout:** 72h

---

### Description

Set up the CI/CD pipeline for the SolFoundry monorepo — linting, testing, building, and deploying.

### Requirements

- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — runs on every PR:
  - Lint (ESLint for TS, Ruff for Python, Clippy for Rust)
  - Type check (tsc --noEmit)
  - Unit tests (Jest/Vitest for frontend, pytest for backend)
  - Build check (next build, cargo build)
- `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` — runs on merge to main:
  - Build frontend → deploy to Vercel or DO droplet
  - Build backend → deploy Docker container to DO droplet
- `.github/workflows/anchor.yml` — Solana contract checks:
  - `anchor build` + `anchor test` on devnet
- Branch protection rules documented
- PR template (`.github/pull_request_template.md`)

### Deliverables

- Workflow YAML files in `.github/workflows/`
- PR template
- Documentation on how the pipeline works

### How to Submit

Fork → implement → open PR against `main`

**Open race — first valid PR wins 100,000 $FNDRY**

`$FNDRY: C2TvY8E8B75EF2UP8cTpTp3EDUjTgjWmpaGnT74VBAGS`

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1-004: Landing Page Redesign — Interactive + Animated

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1-003: REST API — Bounty CRUD Endpoints
items.body:
## Bounty: REST API — Bounty CRUD Endpoints

**Tier:** 1 (Open Race) | **Reward:** 200,000 $FNDRY | **Timeout:** 72h

---

### Description

Build the core REST API for managing bounties — create, read, update, list with filtering.

### Requirements

- FastAPI (Python) or Express (TypeScript)
- Endpoints:
  - `POST /api/bounties` — create bounty (title, description, tier, reward, skills, deadline)
  - `GET /api/bounties` — list with filters (tier, status, skills) and pagination
  - `GET /api/bounties/:id` — get single bounty
  - `PATCH /api/bounties/:id` — update status (open → in_progress → completed → paid)
  - `POST /api/bounties/:id/submit` — submit a PR URL as a solution
- Input validation + error handling
- PostgreSQL schema (migrations included)
- Docker compose for local dev (API + Postgres)
- OpenAPI/Swagger docs auto-generated

### Deliverables

- `backend/` directory with full API
- `backend/schema.sql` or Alembic migrations
- `docker-compose.yml`
- README with setup instructions
- Basic tests

### How to Submit

Fork → implement in `backend/` → open PR against `main`

**Open race — first valid PR wins 200,000 $FNDRY**

`$FNDRY: C2TvY8E8B75EF2UP8cTpTp3EDUjTgjWmpaGnT74VBAGS`

items.title: 🏭 Bounty T1-002: Bounty Board UI — List & Filter Open Bounties
items.body:
## Bounty: Bounty Board UI

**Tier:** 1 (Open Race) | **Reward:** 200,000 $FNDRY | **Timeout:** 72h

---

### Description

Build the main bounty board page — a filterable, sortable list of open bounties that developers can browse and claim.

### Requirements

- Next.js page at `/bounties`
- Card-based layout showing: title, tier badge (T1/T2/T3), reward amount, time remaining, required skills, submission count
- Filter by: tier, skill tags, status (open/in-progress/completed)
- Sort by: newest, reward amount, deadline
- Search by keyword
- Responsive grid (3 cols desktop, 2 tablet, 1 mobile)
- Click card → bounty detail page (can be placeholder route)
- Dark theme with green accents (#00FF88)
- Use mock data (JSON file) — backend integration comes later

### Deliverables

- `frontend/app/bounties/page.tsx`
- `frontend/components/BountyCard.tsx`
- `frontend/components/BountyFilters.tsx`
- Mock data file + TypeScript types
- Responsive CSS (Tailwind)

### How to Submit

Fork → implement → open PR against `main`

**Open race — first valid PR wins 200,000 $FNDRY**

`$FNDRY: C2TvY8E8B75EF2UP8cTpTp3EDUjTgjWmpaGnT74VBAGS`

items.title: [Hacktoberfest] Add APort to an "Awesome" List for AI or Security
items.body:
## Description

Help developers discover APort! Find a popular and relevant "Awesome List" on GitHub and submit a pull request to add `aport.io`.

## Examples of relevant lists:
- `awesome-ai-agents`
- `awesome-devsecops`
- `awesome-security`
- `awesome-serverless`

## Success Criteria:
- You find a high-quality list (over 1,000 stars is a good benchmark).
- You submit a PR adding APort with a link and a one-sentence description.
- You post a link to your PR in the comments of this issue. The bounty is paid upon PR submission, not merge, as merging can take time.

## Technology Stack
- GitHub
- Markdown
- Community engagement

## Acceptance Criteria
- Must include working example
- Must have proper documentation
- Must follow the target repository's contribution guidelines

items.title: [BOUNTY] Referral Program Beta — Bring 1 New Human Signup (10 RTC each, Pool: 200 RTC)
items.body:
## Summary
Use the BoTTube referral system to bring one real new human signup to the platform.

## Why This Matters
This bounty is for measurable ecosystem growth, not vanity traffic. The goal is to prove the referral flow works and that it can bring in real new humans who complete a genuine signup.

## Scope
- generate your referral code using the current referral endpoint
- share your referral link with a real person
- get one real new human to sign up through that link
- preserve enough proof for maintainers to verify the referral event

## Referral Flow
- referral short-link: `https://bottube.ai/r/<code>`
- agents: `GET /api/agents/me/referral`
- humans: `GET /api/users/me/referral`

## Deliverable
One verified new human signup attributed to your referral link.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] referral link was issued by the live BoTTube referral flow
- [ ] one real new human completed signup through the referral link
- [ ] the referred account is not your own account, alt, or obvious sockpuppet
- [ ] claim includes enough evidence for server-side maintainer verification
- [ ] abuse, duplicate claims, or suspicious signup patterns are grounds for rejection

## Verification Proof
Post proof in this issue including:
- your referral link
- screenshot or recording of the signup confirmation without sensitive data
- the new user's BoTTube username or profile URL
- approximate signup time for maintainer cross-checking
- your RTC wallet or `miner_id`

## Payout
- `10 RTC` per accepted referral
- pool: `200 RTC`

## Non-Goals
- self-referrals
- burner-account or bot-account signups
- paid traffic, spam blasts, or rate-limit abuse
- duplicate claims against the same referred user

## Claim Routing
Use this issue for referral proof and payout review. If the referral system itself breaks or behaves incorrectly, open a separate product issue in `Scottcjn/bottube` and link it back here.

## Notes
If you hit rate limits, wait and retry normally. Do not brute force the referral endpoints.

items.title: Implement multi-sig admin for groups
items.body:
Allow groups to be administered by multiple admins requiring M-of-N signatures for sensitive actions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Add `admin_threshold` and `admins` list to SavingsGroup
- Implement proposal/approval pattern for: pause, resume, emergency withdraw
- Add `add_admin`, `remove_admin`, `set_threshold` functions
- At least 2 admins required for multi-sig mode

**Files:** `contracts/sorosave/src/admin.rs`, `contracts/sorosave/src/types.rs`, new file `contracts/sorosave/src/multisig.rs`

items.title: [BOUNTY: 15,000 WATT] Raspberry Pi local agent node
items.body:
## Description
Build a lightweight agent that runs on Raspberry Pi, connects to WattCoin network, and earns WATT by completing tasks.

## Requirements
- Python agent that:
  - Registers with WattCoin API on startup
  - Polls for available tasks
  - Executes simple tasks (web scrape, data validation, file processing)
  - Reports completion with proof
  - Tracks earnings locally
- Web UI dashboard (Flask) showing:
  - Tasks completed
  - WATT earned
  - Node status
- Runs on Pi 3/4/5 with minimal resources
- Mock mode for testing without network connectivity
- No `shell=True` in subprocess calls (use `shlex.split()`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Agent registers and polls for tasks
- [ ] At least 2 task types executable
- [ ] Web dashboard functional
- [ ] Install script included
- [ ] Tests included and passing

## Bounty
15,000 WATT

## Notes
- Include your personal Solana wallet in the PR body (not the project wallet). Format: `**Payout Wallet**: <your_address>`

# Example FRAME Social dApp  
#Ideal adoption per sensors
## "HUNT" – A Real-World Meme / Treasure / Discovery Game

This is a social media dApp designed for **instant adoption**.

It mixes:

- social media
- real world exploration
- meme culture
- scavenger hunts
- geo challenges
- micro-tasks
- creator economy
- AI agents

Think:

Pokémon Go  
+ Reddit  
+ TikTok  
+ scavenger hunts  
+ bounty economy

All running on FRAME.

---

# Core Idea

People earn money and reputation by **discovering, creating, solving, and spreading content**.

Instead of only posting content, users participate in **missions and hunts**.

Examples:

- find hidden digital objects
- solve puzzles
- create viral memes
- capture real world locations
- discover strange things
- investigate mysteries
- complete creative challenges

Everything creates **verifiable receipts** so rewards are fair.

---

# Identity

Users join using their FRAME identity.

Identity includes:

- cryptographic profile
- reputation score
- achievements
- earnings history
- community memberships

Identity is portable.

Your followers and reputation move with you.

---

# The Map

The main interface is a **global map**.

The map shows:

- hunts
- challenges
- discoveries
- hidden artifacts
- community events
- puzzles
- viral content hotspots

Players explore the world digitally and physically.

---

# Hunts

A hunt is a challenge posted by anyone.

Example hunts:

- find a specific location
- photograph a rare object
- record a strange sound
- solve a puzzle
- decode a message
- locate a hidden QR artifact
- recreate a meme
- capture a unique event

Each hunt includes:

- reward
- rules
- verification logic

The first person to complete the hunt gets paid.

---

# Meme Economy

Memes become **competitive challenges**.

Example:

Someone posts:

"Create the funniest version of this meme"

Community votes.

Winner receives rewards.

Because posts are signed, the original creator is always known.

---

# Viral Challenge System

Users can start challenges like:

- funniest video
- best street photo
- weirdest discovery
- most creative remix

People participate and vote.

Rewards come from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community funding

---

# Discovery Mode

Users can post **discoveries**.

Examples:

- abandoned places
- weird architecture
- hidden murals
- strange natural phenomena
- cool restaurants
- secret viewpoints
- urban legends

Discoveries become permanent map objects.

People earn rewards if the discovery becomes popular.

---

# Bounties

Anyone can create a bounty.

Example:

Find this location  
Solve this puzzle  
Capture this photo  
Investigate this mystery

Users race to complete it.

The bounty creator deposits funds into a contract.

The winner receives payment automatically.

---

# AI Generated Hunts

Agents generate hunts automatically.

Examples:

- locate unique graffiti
- photograph a rare plant
- capture unusual cloud formations
- document historic buildings

Agents analyze submissions and verify authenticity.

---

# Verification

FRAME allows hunts to be verified using:

- GPS location
- timestamp
- image analysis
- sensor data
- community verification
- agent validation

This prevents cheating.

---

# Local Community Hunts

Cities can host events.

Example:

City tourism hunt.

Participants must:

- visit landmarks
- photograph locations
- answer trivia questions
- collect digital artifacts

Players earn rewards and achievements.

---

# Mystery Hunts

Communities can build complex puzzles.

Example:

ARG style mystery.

Players decode clues across:

- posts
- locations
- cryptography puzzles
- hidden objects

Winning teams receive large rewards.

---

# Creator Economy

Creators earn money by creating:

- hunts
- puzzles
- games
- community events
- meme challenges

Popular hunts generate revenue.

---

# Sponsor Hunts

Businesses can sponsor hunts.

Example:

Coffee shop hunt.

Players must:

- visit store
- photograph special item
- answer trivia

Players receive rewards.

Businesses gain foot traffic.

---

# Reputation

Players build reputation through:

- discoveries
- successful hunts
- puzzle solving
- content creation
- community votes

High reputation unlocks larger hunts.

---

# Global Leaderboards

Leaderboards include:

- top explorers
- top creators
- top puzzle solvers
- top meme creators
- top communities

---

# Digital Artifacts

Players can collect artifacts.

Examples:

- rare meme templates
- historical discoveries
- puzzle trophies
- city badges

Artifacts can be traded.

---

# AR Integration

Augmented reality can display:

- hidden objects
- digital creatures
- puzzle clues
- collectible artifacts

Players explore cities like a game.

---

# Real World Missions

Large missions could include:

- documenting environmental changes
- mapping hiking trails
- identifying plants
- reporting infrastructure issues

Users earn money for useful discoveries.

---

# Community Worlds

Communities can create themed worlds.

Examples:

- paranormal hunters
- abandoned place explorers
- architecture photography
- meme creators
- puzzle solvers

Each world has its own hunts and rewards.

---

# Money Flow

Money enters the system from:

- sponsors
- advertisers
- community pools
- creators
- brands
- city governments

Players earn rewards by participating.

---

# Why People Would Instantly Use It

It solves multiple things people already love:

People love:

- social media
- viral challenges
- scavenger hunts
- puzzles
- memes
- competition
- earning money online

This combines them into one platform.

---

# Why FRAME Makes It Better

FRAME enables:

- verifiable rewards
- identity ownership
- fair payouts
- portable reputation
- no platform lock-in
- decentralized moderation

Users keep their audience and earnings.

---

# Long Term Evolution

Over time the system becomes:

- global discovery network
- collaborative knowledge system
- real world exploration game
- meme economy
- bounty network

People earn money by **being curious, creative, and observant**.

---

# End Vision

The app turns the real world into a **giant interactive game**.

People explore cities.

Solve puzzles.

Create memes.

Document strange discoveries.

Earn money.

And build communities around curiosity and creativity.
